mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.0% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 6.1% is elevated relative to pre-pandemic levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer spending momentum and aggregate demand strength. Accelerating retail sales over the preceding month signal robust economic activity that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side economic strength. When INDPRO accelerates above its 3-month trend, it signals expanding productive capacity and positive earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong industrial production growth, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate jointly determine labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth decelerates while UNRATE rises simultaneously, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 137473.0 thousand persons, UNRATE at 6.1%
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with employment deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances (both conditions met), or interaction Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors. When both accelerate simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand strength that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 965.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 381169.0 million dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation pressure (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that raises real discount rates and compresses equity valuations. When CPI growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains above 2.5%, it produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 219.181 index, FEDFUNDS at 2.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) represents the broadest measure of economic activity. In late-cycle expansions where GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2% annualized while remaining positive, equity valuations compress as growth expectations reset downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% but remains positive
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11740.3 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite sub-2% GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.
